features.featureTitle: Build a complete Admin Dashboard page at the `/admin` route, protected by the existing `AdminGuard` component. The dashboard must contain clearly separated sections reachable via a sidebar or tab navigation: Overview/Analytics, User Management, Book Management, Storage Control, Platform Settings, and Security. The UI must be clean, modern, and fully mobile-responsive.

Implement the Analytics/Overview section of the Admin Dashboard. Display stat cards for: total registered users, total books, total uploads, total approved books, total pending books. Display a list of the most-viewed books and most-bookmarked books. Display most-active users by upload count.

Implement the User Management section of the Admin Dashboard. Show a paginated or scrollable table of all registered users with columns: display name, principal ID, role, status (active/banned/suspended), books uploaded, join date. Provide action buttons per row to: delete user, ban/unban user, suspend/unsuspend user. Confirm destructive actions with a confirmation dialog before calling the backend.

Implement the Book Management section of the Admin Dashboard. Show a searchable, filterable list of all books (including pending and approved) with cover thumbnail, title, author, genre, status (approved/pending/rejected), featured flag, and view count. Provide per-book actions: edit (title, description, genre, cover URL), delete, approve, reject, feature/unfeature. The admin upload form should reuse or mirror the existing Upload page form.

Implement the Security section of the Admin Dashboard. Display a list of currently banned and suspended users with their status and the ability to unban/unsuspend from this view. Add a searchable account-blocking form where the admin can enter a principal ID to block (ban) that account. Display a platform activity log showing recent events: new user registrations, book uploads, and books removed — with timestamps and actor principal.
